title: Travel for Free
subline: How to use miles, points, and credit-card rewards to fly anywhere on someone else's dime.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A world route map: a single curved hairline path from NEW YORK (left) to TOKYO (right), with city markers at intervals. The arrival dot (TOKYO) is RED. Title sits below. -->
  <g transform="translate(160 600)" fill="none" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif">
    <!-- Latitude grid lines (faint) -->
    <g stroke="#1a1a1a" stroke-width="1">
      <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="1280" y2="0"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="120" x2="1280" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="240" x2="1280" y2="240"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="360" x2="1280" y2="360"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="480" x2="1280" y2="480"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="600" x2="1280" y2="600"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The great-circle route: a sweeping curve from (0, 240) up to (1280, 240) via the poles -->
    <path d="M 0 240 Q 640 -160 1280 240" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- City markers at intervals along the route -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1.5" fill="#0a0a0a">
      <circle cx="0" cy="240" r="8"/>
      <circle cx="213" cy="160" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="426" cy="40" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="640" cy="-60" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="853" cy="40" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="1066" cy="160" r="6"/>
    </g>
    <!-- City labels under the markers -->
    <g font-size="11" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="0.18em">
      <text x="0" y="280">NEW YORK</text>
      <text x="213" y="200">REYKJAVÍK</text>
      <text x="426" y="80">OSLO</text>
      <text x="640" y="-30">N. POLE</text>
      <text x="853" y="80">ANCHORAGE</text>
      <text x="1066" y="200">PETROPAVLOVSK</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Final arrival marker — RED, larger -->
    <circle cx="1280" cy="240" r="14" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <text x="1280" y="285" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="0.2em">TOKYO</text>
    <!-- A small airplane icon (chevron) along the route -->
    <g transform="translate(640 -10) rotate(0)" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2" fill="none">
      <path d="M 0 -10 L 30 0 L 0 10 L 10 0 Z" fill="#0a0a0a"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Distance label at the bottom of the route map -->
    <text x="640" y="600" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.3em">10,800 KM · $0</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: The book IS a great-circle route map. A sweeping curve from New York to Tokyo over the North Pole — the polar route, the cheapest distance in miles. Six city markers in graphite, the final destination (Tokyo) in RED. A small chevron-airplane midway along the route. Title sits in the bottom 1/3, weight 500 command, mixed-case. Brand 'MILEAGE RUN' in the top-left; pill '0$' at the top — the actual cost. Distance label at the bottom of the map: '10,800 KM · $0'.
